rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation annexes in the component implementation scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, and connections as the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
anti_patterns: Do not remove generated clauses without diagnostic justification
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AADL property names and unit tokens in AGREE expressions
